objects.name: Discretization Error
source_spans: Smaller steps usually reduce discretization error but require more updates over a fixed time interval.

objects.name: Euler Predictor
source_spans: It first forms the Euler predictor y_{n+1} = y_n + hF(t_n,y_n)

objects.name: First-Order Equation
source_spans: A first-order equation can be written as y'(t) = F(t,y(t)).

objects.name: Forward Euler Method
source_spans: The forward Euler method is applied to an initial value problem by advancing from the current approximation along the current vector-field slope.

objects.name: Forward Euler Update
source_spans: y_{n+1} = y_n + hF(t_n,y_n)

objects.name: Heun's Method
source_spans: Heun's method extends the forward Euler method with a predictor-corrector step.

objects.name: Heun's Update
source_spans: y_{n+1} = y_n + \frac{h}{2}\left(F(t_n,y_n)+F(t_n+h,\widetilde{y}_{n+1})\right)

objects.name: Initial Value Problem
source_spans: An initial value problem combines an ordinary differential equation with an initial condition such as y(t_0)=y_0.

objects.name: Ordinary Differential Equation
source_spans: An ordinary differential equation relates an unknown function of one variable to its derivatives.

objects.name: Step Size
source_spans: The step size h sets the spacing between consecutive numerical time points.

objects.name: Vector Field
source_spans: The vector field F specifies the slope that the solution should follow at each state.

objects.name: A* Search
source_spans: A* search extends Dijkstra's algorithm by using a heuristic function \(h(v)\) to guide which vertex is explored next.

objects.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
source_spans: Dijkstra's algorithm is applied to this problem when all edge weights are nonnegative.

objects.name: Edge Relaxation
source_spans: it applies edge relaxation to each outgoing edge.

objects.name: Heuristic Function
source_spans: heuristic function \(h(v)\)

objects.name: Path
source_spans: A path is a sequence of compatible directed edges.

objects.name: Path Cost
source_spans: Its path cost is the sum of their edge weights

objects.name: Relaxation Update
source_spans: d(v) \leftarrow \min\{d(v),\, d(u)+w(u,v)\}

objects.name: Single-Source Shortest-Path Problem
source_spans: The single-source shortest-path problem asks for a minimum-cost path from a chosen source vertex to every reachable vertex.

objects.name: Weighted Directed Graph
source_spans: A weighted directed graph assigns a numerical weight \(w(u,v)\) to each directed edge \((u,v)\).

objects.name: Bisection method
source_spans: The bisection method begins with a sign-changing bracket \([a,b]\) satisfying \(f(a)f(b)<0\).

objects.name: Damped Newton method
source_spans: The damped Newton method extends Newton's method by scaling the Newton correction with a factor between zero and one.

objects.name: Midpoint formula
source_spans: m = \frac{a+b}{2}

objects.name: Newton update formula
source_spans: x_{k+1} = x_k - \frac{f(x_k)}{f'(x_k)}

objects.name: Newton's method
source_spans: Newton's method uses the derivative to replace the function locally by its tangent line.

objects.name: Root-finding problem
source_spans: A root-finding problem asks for a value \(x^*\) at which a scalar function is zero

objects.name: Sign-changing bracket
source_spans: sign-changing bracket \([a,b]\) satisfying \(f(a)f(b)<0\)

objects.name: Likelihood
source_spans: The likelihood is defined from this statistical model by treating the observations as fixed and comparing possible parameter values.

objects.name: Likelihood Function
source_spans: L(\theta; x_{1:n}) = \prod_{i=1}^{n} p(x_i \mid \theta).

objects.name: Log-Likelihood
source_spans: The log-likelihood is obtained by taking the logarithm of the likelihood.

objects.name: Log-Likelihood Function
source_spans: \ell(\theta) = \log L(\theta; x_{1:n}) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} \log p(x_i \mid \theta).

objects.name: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
source_spans: Maximum likelihood estimation uses the log-likelihood as its objective and selects a parameter value that maximizes it

objects.name: MLE Estimator
source_spans: \widehat{\theta}_{\mathrm{MLE}} = \operatorname*{arg\,max}_{\theta}\, \ell(\theta).

objects.name: Newton's Method
source_spans: Newton's method can be applied within maximum likelihood estimation to compute a candidate numerically.

objects.name: Score Equation
source_spans: At an interior differentiable optimum, an MLE candidate satisfies the score equation \(s(\theta)=0\).

objects.name: Score Function
source_spans: The score function is the derivative of the log-likelihood with respect to the parameter

objects.name: Score Function Formula
source_spans: s(\theta) = \nabla_{\theta}\ell(\theta).

objects.name: Statistical Model
source_spans: A statistical model is a family of probability distributions indexed by an unknown parameter \(\theta\).
